title: Cover for the Big Risks
scenario: You already have a basic health policy, but a colleague's cancer treatment recently drained their family's savings despite hospital cover, because of income loss and non-hospital costs. A critical illness rider costs about ₹800 a month and pays a lump sum on diagnosis of major illnesses. Worth it?

choices.label: Add critical illness cover to bridge the gap your hospital policy leaves
choices.lesson: Critical illness cover pays a lump sum on diagnosis, replacing lost income and costs that a standard health policy never touches.

choices.label: Skip it for now and spend the money on a gadget upgrade
choices.lesson: A serious diagnosis can wipe out years of savings; skipping affordable protection is a gamble against your own future earnings.
